REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, DEFENDANT PH ARCHITECTS, LLC MOTION FOR DETERMINATION OF GOOD FAITH SETTLEMENT IS DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE. THE COURT WAS NOT PROVIDED WITH SUFFICIENT INFORMATION TO CONDUCT THE ANALYSIS UNDER TECH-BUILT, E.G. A ROUGH APPROXIMATION OF PLAINTIFF'S TOTAL RECOVERY, TO DETERMINE WHETHER THE SETTLEMENT AMOUNT IS GROSSLY DISPROPORTIONATE TO THE SETTLLOR'S FAIR SHARE OF ANTICIPATED DAMAGES. SEE TECH-BILT, INC. V. WOODWARD-CLYDE & ASSOCIATES, (1985) 38 CAL.3D 488; ABBOTT FORD, INC. V. SUPERIOR COURT, (1987) 43 CAL. 3D 858. THE COURT FURTHER NOTES THAT NO INFORMATION WAS PROVIDED FOR THE COURT TO DETERMINE NOW (OR AT TRIAL) WHAT THE ALLOCATION OF THE SETTLEMENT AMOUNT WAS. THIS CASE INVOLVES ALLEGATIONS AGAINST MULTIPLE JOINT TORTFEASORS THAT ARE NOT SIMILARLY SITUATED AND WHOSE POTENTIAL LIABILITY DOES NOT COMPLETELY OVERLAP. WHILE THE MOVING PARTIES (SEE JOINDER) SUPPLY THE COURT WITH A VERY SPECIFIC NUMBER ($527,473) IN CLAIMED DAMAGES TO PLAINTIFFS FOR THE SERVICES THAT PH PROVIDED FOR THE GROUND-UP REMODEL AT THE PROPERTY, THEY PROVIDE NO EXPLANATION AS TO HOW PLAINTIFF ARRIVED AT THIS NUMBER, I.E. WHICH DAMAGES ARE INCLUDED.PREVAILING PARTY TO SUBMIT PROPOSED ORDER THAT IS IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312(B).JUDGE: CHARLES F. HAINES; CLERK: K. VALDES; PROCEEDINGS REPORTED BY ZINA BAILEY; CSR#11267; (510)710-5883; ZINABAILEY@YAHOO.COM.(D501)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Determination Of Good Faith Settlement
Off Calendar Apr-01-2026 Continued To Apr-30-26 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 501
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:

Rosanna Fristed; 949-870-3301; Miller Morton Caillat & Nevis LLP; counsel for plaintiffs MHYF Holdings, LLC, Matthew Humphrey and Ying Fu.
Matthew Becker, Esq. for defendant Arch Plumbing, Inc.
Frank Chatzipantsios, Esq. for defendant PH Architects, LLC.

Hearing held. Ms. Fristed contests the continuance date of April 30, 2026 and requests matter be heard sooner if there is an available date. The court is unable to continue to a sooner date. Not argued. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF MHYF HOLDINGS, LLC, A DELAWARE LIMITED LIABILITY COMPANY, MATTHEW HUMPHREY, YING FU, QUITE CAPITAL MANAGEMENT, L.P. Notice Of Motion And Motion For Determination Of Good Faith Settlement is continued to April 30, 2026 for parties to submit supplemental briefs as follows:

(1) plaintiff to submit a brief, not to exceed 2 pages addressing whether its motion is seeking relief identical and/or similar to the relief sought in PPH Architects LLC's later-filed motion; and
(2) if one of the motions is granted, will the other motion become moot or there will be outstanding issues remaining (a) if Plaintiff's motion is granted first; or (b) if Defendant's motion is granted first.
(3) opposing defendants submit a supplemental brief limited to citations to authority (no argument) authorizing filing of an opposition (to plaintiff's motion) 5 days after the noticed hearing date without leave of Court; or
(b) attach a copy of a filed endorsed order obtained prior to March 18, 2026 authorizing the filing of opposition to Plaintiff's motion. There shall be no authorities or discussion regarding filing and timeliness of opposition to Defendant PH Architects LLC's motion.
Supplemental briefs to be filed and served no later than April 17, 2026. There shall be no additional filings in support of or in opposition to this motion.

DEFENDANTS CHRIS KELLY, INDIVIDUALLY AND AS CO-TRUSTEE OF THE KELLY FAMILY TRUST, and LARA KELLY's MOTION TO QUASH SUBPOENAS.

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Determination Of Good Faith Settlement; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Thereof; And Declaration Of Catherine L. Deter In Support Thereof
Off Calendar Jan-15-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances
Rosanna Fristed, Esq. for Plaintiffs MHYF Holdings, LLC, Matthew Humphrey, Ying Fu and Quite Capital Management, LP
Sarah Marsey, Esq. and co-counsel Ana Dragojevic, Esq. for Defendant Chris Kelly and Lara Gay Kelly.
Patrick Cronican, Esq. for Defendant Cal-Cade Construction.
John Leary, Esq. for Defendant GFDS Engineers.
Matthew Becker, Esq. for Defendant Arch Plumbing, Inc.
Josh Friedman, Esq. specially appearing counsel for Defendant Xie Associates
Frank Chatzipantsios, Esq. for Defenant PH Architects, LLC

Hearing held.

Mr. Chatzipantsios for Defendant PH Architects requests clarification and requesting the court what the defendant can provide for the court to grant the motion. Mr. Chatzipantsios for Defendant PH Architects further request to continue the motion for defendant to submit supplemental briefing.

The Court denies this request, Counsel may re-file as the ruling is without prejudice.

Ms.Fristed for Plaintiff MHYF Holdings offers to have an expert declaration filed.

The Court heard the arguments and requests, the Court adopts the tentative rulings as follows:
DEFENDANT PH ARCHITECTS, LLC Motion For Determination Of Good Faith Settlement;

is DENIED without prejudice. The Court was not provided with sufficient information to conduct the analysis under Tech-Built, e.g. a rough approximation of plaintiff's total recovery, to determine whether the settlement amount is grossly disproportionate to the settllor's fair share of anticipated damages. See Tech-Bilt, Inc. v. Woodward-Clyde & Associates, (1985) 38 Cal.3d 488; Abbott Ford, Inc. v. Superior Court, (1987) 43 Cal. 3d 858. The Court further notes that no information was provided for the Court to determine now (or at trial) what the allocation of the settlement amount was. This case involves allegations against multiple joint tortfeasors that are not similarly situated and whose potential liability does not completely overlap. While the moving parties (see joinder) supply the Court with a very specific number ($527,473) in claimed damages to Plaintiffs for the services that PH provided for the ground-up remodel at the Property, they provide no explanation as to how Plaintiff arrived at this number, i.e. which damages are included.

Prevailing Party to submit proposed order that is in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b).

Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: K. Valdes; Proceedings reported by Zina Bailey; CSR#11267; (510)710-5883; zinabailey@yahoo.com.(D501)

Off Calendar Mar-26-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANTS CHRIS KELLY INDIVIDUALLY AND AS CO-TRUSTEE, LARA KELLY'S DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFFS' 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT.

Appearances: Sarah A. Marsey and Isabella Granucci of Holland & Knight LLP (415.743.6900) for Defendants and moving parties; Rosanna E. Fristed of Miller, Morton, Caillat & Nevis, LLP (949.870.3301) for Plaintiffs and responding parties.

Argued and the court modifies its ruling as follows: Defendants Kellys' Demurrer to Amended Complaint is SUSTAINED. Demurrer is sustained with leave to amend from Plaintiff to allege fraud and non-disclosure causes of action with requisite particularity as to each representation/concealment/failure to disclose connecting them to reliance and resulting damages.

Demurrer is sustained without leave to amend as to the cause of action for breach of covenant of good faith and fair dealing. Demurrer is sustained without leave to amend as to Plaintiffs Humphrey and Fu's cause of action for breach of contract. Contrary to Plaintiff's statement made on March 3, 2025, in opposition, there is no dismissal on file as of the time of this ruling and there was no substantive opposition to the demurrer.

Demurrer as to the cause of action for professional negligence is sustained with leave to amend for Plaintiffs to allege facts giving rise to liability. The Court notes that opposition contained no cases factually similar to this action holding the owners of the property vicariously liable for hired engineer's professional negligence.

Demurrer to claims brought individually by Humphrey and Fu is sustained with leave to amend to allege facts supporting standing. Order signed. Judge: Harry L. Jacobs, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Not Reported (501/HLJ)

DEMURRER to CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jul-30-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances (by Zoom):
Rosanna Fristed, Esq. 949-870-3301 Miller Morton Caillat & Nevis, LLP for Plaintiff.
Frank Chatzipantsios, Esq. 949-757-4557 Wood Smith Henning & Berman LLP for Defendant PH Architects.
Jacqueline Harvey, Esq. 415.743.6900 HOLLAND & KNIGHT LLP for Defendants Chris Kelly et al.

RULING - The matter is not contested. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

2-CROSS DEFENDANT CHRIS KELLY INDIV AND AS CO-TRUSTEE OF THE KELLY FAMILY TRUST, LARA KELLY's DEMURRER to CROSS COMPLAINT.

SUSTAINED in part and OVERRULED in part. As an initial matter the Demurrer to Neill Bassi's Cross-Complaint is sustained with leave to amend to allege standing to bring all/any causes of action as a signatory to the agreement or a third-party beneficiary.

Demurrer is SUSTAINED with leave to amend as to the cause of action for breach of contract for the Cross-Complaint(s) to to allege facts (i) in support of "the breach of contract that occurred when the Kellys filed their cross-complaint attains Broker," (ii) and/or facts supporting the claim that Kellys "fail[ed] to indemnify, defend, and hold Broker harmless' by identifying the claims for which there was failure to (1) indemnify; (2) defend; and/or (3) hold harmless; and/or facts showing that there was a pending or threatened proceeding during the relevant times per Listing Agreement paragraph 6(iv).

Demurrer is OVERRULED as to the Express Indemnity and Declaratory Relief causes of action as the indemnity clause is susceptible to interpretation urged by Cross-Complainant. (Zalkind v. Ceradyne, Inc. (2011) 194 Cal.App.4th 1010, 1025.)

Demurrer is SUSTAINED with leave to amend as to the cause of action for implied indemnity for the Cross-Complainant to allege facts supporting the claim that there can be any liability stemming from the Buyers' operative complaint against the Brokers, i.e. identify the claims in the complaint asserted against the Broker for which "a judgment be rendered against Broker for damages allegedly sustained by Buyers." (Cross-Complaint, par. 41.)

Prevailing party to submit a proposed order that is verbatim with the Court's ruling and in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Charles F. Haines, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (501/cfh)
